title: calyptronoma plumeriana
intro: Calyptronoma plumeriana, also known as the "prayer plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its use in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Calyptronoma plumeriana include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, with the leaves and stems being used for their medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", calyptronoma plumeriana exhibits immunostimulatory activity, with the insoluble fraction showing a significant dose-dependent immunostimulatory activity that could be used as an immunostimulatory agent in humans.
